purpose: Explains the sign of stock-bond correlation as a function of the dominant macroeconomic shock type (demand vs. supply) mediated by the central bank reaction function
components: Central bank reaction function (Taylor Rule coefficient φ_π)
Phillips Curve slope as mediating parameter
Inflation expectations anchoring as boundary condition
current_assessment: All eight agents anchor on this framework. Current ambiguous shock mix (residual supply-side inflation + demand softening) places the US in the framework's indeterminate zone. The effective Taylor coefficient has diverged from textbook values (1.0-1.2 vs. 1.5-2.0), and fiscal dominance may make the reaction function endogenous — a modification but not a falsification of the framework.
limitations: Assumes exogenous central bank reaction function; fiscal dominance makes it endogenous
Phillips Curve slope (0.1-0.5) is a critical parameter the literature cannot pin down
Does not incorporate fiscal dynamics as an independent channel
Maturity-dependent correlation bifurcation requires extending the framework
